title: Athyrium crinitum
taxonomy: Athyrium crinitum is a species of fern in the family Woodsiaceae. This fern is native to China and other parts of Asia. It belongs to the genus Athyrium, which includes various species of ferns. Athyrium crinitum is typically found in shaded, humid environments. Its classification is further supported by its membership in the subclass Polypodiidae.

intro: Athyrium crinitum, commonly known as the Lady fern, is a species of flowering plant in the fern family (Athyriaceae) native to Asia. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral activities, making it a potential remedy for various health issues such as fever, cough, and digestive problems. The bioactive constituents of Athyrium crinitum include flavonoids, saponins, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. In traditional medicine, the plant is often used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are used to treat respiratory and gastrointestinal disorders.
